rulings.Case Title: IN RE: KIM UNTIEDT CONANT AND KAREN U. HILLMAN, AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Transfer Venure To Marin County
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 19, 2014, line 2. RESPONDENT ELISEO TWO, LLC and JAZO GROUP LLC's Motion To Transfer Venue To Marin County GRANTED. Respondent's request for attorney fees and costs is DENIED. Petitioners do not dispute that the Respondents' principal places of business are located in Marin County. The Respondents did not show that they attempted to stipulate to change of venue and were rejected, or that the Petitioners selected the present venue in bad faith. Venue is not proper in San Francisco. The request for fees is denied. (Code of Civ. Proc., § 396b(b). = (501/MJM) - late tentative

rulings.Case Title: ADIL SHAIKH VS. EDUARDA RODRIGUEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Waive Objections, To Continue The Trial Date, For Conditional Terminating Sanctions, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 19, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT EDUARDA RODRIGUEZ's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Waive Objections, To Continue The Trial Date, For Conditional Terminating Sanctions, And For Monetary Sanctions GRANTED. Plaintiff shall respond to the request for production of documents and produce documents by February 20, 2014 at 1:30pm. No opposition filed. Objections are waived. (Code of Civ. Proc., § 2031.300(a).) Plaintiff SHALL PAY SANCTIONS to Defendant's attorney in the amount of $700. (Code of Civ. Proc., § 2031.300(c).) Trial will be continued to March 10, 2014. = (501/MJM) - late tentative

rulings.Case Title: EPMI, ABAYSIDE COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JOE NICK VIDURRI et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 19, 2014, line 7. DEFENDANT JOE VIDURRI's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff has 10 days to file an amended complaint from the filing of the Court's order. The Complaint and attached notice to quit fail to identify the provisions of the lease that Plaintiff claims that Defendant breached and/or whether the unlawful detainer action is solely based on a tort for nuisance. Plaintiff must clarify the uncertainty of under what theories the allegations proceed. Plaintiff must include allegations explaining how and why Defendant is responsible for the incident. Additionally, Attachment 15 and the notice to quit contradict each other-it is unclear under what law Plaintiff is proceeding. The notice states that the tenancy is being terminated pursuant to San Francisco Rent Ordinance § 37.9(a)(3). In Attachment 15 to the Complaint, however, Plaintiff provides that the tenancy is exempt from San Francisco Administrative Code §§ 37.2(r)(4)(A) and (B). = (501/MJM) - late tentative
